title: Ada and the Magic School Library
synopsis: When Ada discovers that storybook characters are escaping from the school library books, she must help the librarian solve the magical mystery before the fairy tale world gets too mixed up.

pages.text: Ada loved school. She loved her teacher, Ms. Chen. She loved her friends Carlos and Maya. But today felt different. Very different. Ada walked into Sunshine Elementary School. Something was not right. A small glass slipper sat on the front steps. Ada picked it up. "This looks like Cinderella's shoe," she said to herself.

illustration.description: Ada, a young Black girl with curly hair in two puff ponytails, wearing a bright purple backpack and yellow dress, stands at colorful school entrance stairs holding a tiny sparkling glass slipper. The school building is cheerful with rainbow decorations and morning sunlight streaming through.
illustration.alt: Ada holding a glass slipper at school entrance

pages.text: Ada walked down the hallway. Her friend Carlos ran up to her. "Ada! Ada! You won't believe what I saw!" Carlos was very excited. His dark eyes were wide. "I saw three bears in the cafeteria! They were eating porridge!" Ada looked at Carlos. "That's silly, Carlos. Bears don't go to school." But then Ada remembered the glass slipper.

illustration.description: Carlos, a Latino boy with short black hair wearing a green striped shirt, gestures excitedly to Ada in a bright school hallway with colorful lockers and student artwork on the walls. His expression shows amazement and wonder.
illustration.alt: Carlos telling Ada about the bears in the school hallway

pages.text: Maya joined them at their classroom door. "Did you guys see the giant beanstalk?" she asked. Maya had long black hair and wore glasses. "It's growing right through the art room window!" Ada, Carlos, and Maya looked at each other. Something magical was happening at Sunshine Elementary. They needed to find out what it was.

illustration.description: Maya, an Asian girl with long straight black hair and round glasses wearing a pink sweater, points toward a classroom window where green vines can be seen growing. Ada and Carlos look surprised and curious in the colorful school hallway.
illustration.alt: Maya showing Ada and Carlos the beanstalk through the window

pages.text: The three friends went to find their teacher. Ms. Chen was in the library talking to Mr. Rodriguez, the librarian. Mr. Rodriguez looked worried. His usually neat gray hair was messy. "The storybook characters keep jumping out of the books!" he said. "First it was Goldilocks. Then the Three Little Pigs. Now Jack from the beanstalk!"

illustration.description: Ms. Chen, an Asian woman with short brown hair wearing a blue cardigan, stands with Mr. Rodriguez, a Hispanic man with gray hair and glasses wearing a brown vest, in a magical library where books float open and sparkles emerge from their pages.
illustration.alt: Ms. Chen and Mr. Rodriguez in the magical library with floating books

pages.text: Ada stepped forward. "We can help!" she said bravely. "We already found some clues." She showed them the glass slipper. Carlos told them about the three bears. Maya told them about the beanstalk. Mr. Rodriguez smiled a little. "You three are very observant," he said. "But I don't know how to get them back into their books."

illustration.description: Ada confidently holds up the sparkling glass slipper while Carlos and Maya stand beside her in the enchanted library. Books with glowing pages surround them, and magical sparkles float in the air. The mood is adventurous and determined.
illustration.alt: Ada showing the glass slipper to the adults with her friends

pages.text: Ada had an idea. "Let's follow the characters!" she said. "Maybe they want to go home too." The friends walked to the cafeteria first. Sure enough, Papa Bear, Mama Bear, and Baby Bear were there. They looked sad. "This porridge is too salty," said Papa Bear. "We miss our forest home," said Mama Bear.

illustration.description: Three cartoon bears sit sadly at a bright cafeteria table with colorful lunch trays. Papa Bear is large and brown, Mama Bear wears a floral apron, and Baby Bear is small and cute. The cafeteria has cheerful yellow walls and lunch tables.
illustration.alt: Three sad bears eating in the school cafeteria

pages.text: Next, they found Goldilocks in the art room. She was tangled up in Jack's giant beanstalk! "Help me!" she called. "I just wanted to see what was up here!" Jack was at the top of the beanstalk, looking scared. "I can't get down!" he yelled. "This isn't my story!" Ada realized the characters were all mixed up in the wrong places.

illustration.description: Goldilocks, a girl with curly blonde hair in a blue dress, is wrapped in thick green beanstalk vines in a bright art room. Jack, a boy in simple brown clothes, waves from high up among the leaves. The scene is chaotic but colorful with art supplies scattered around.
illustration.alt: Goldilocks tangled in the beanstalk with Jack at the top

pages.text: In the hallway, they met Cinderella. She was hopping on one foot, looking for her shoe. "Have you seen my glass slipper?" she asked sadly. Ada smiled and gave it back to her. "Thank you!" said Cinderella. "But I don't know how to get back to my castle." Ada was starting to understand the problem. The characters were lost!

illustration.description: Cinderella, a young woman with brown skin and black hair in an elegant bun wearing a shimmery blue ballgown, hops on one foot in the school hallway. Ada hands her the sparkling glass slipper with a kind smile. The hallway is bright with student artwork on the walls.
illustration.alt: Ada returning the glass slipper to Cinderella in the hallway

pages.text: Ada, Carlos, and Maya ran back to the library. "Mr. Rodriguez!" Ada called. "The characters are all mixed up! They want to go home, but they don't know which books are theirs!" Mr. Rodriguez nodded. "You're right, Ada. But how do we show them the way?" Ada thought hard. Then she snapped her fingers. "We read their stories out loud!"

illustration.description: Ada has a lightbulb moment with her finger raised and eyes bright with excitement in the magical library. Carlos and Maya look amazed beside her while Mr. Rodriguez listens attentively. Books continue to float and sparkle around them in the enchanted space.
illustration.alt: Ada having a brilliant idea in the library

pages.text: "That's brilliant!" said Mr. Rodriguez. "If they hear their own stories, they'll remember where they belong!" Ms. Chen clapped her hands. "Let's gather all the characters in the library," she said. "Ada, you can be our story leader." Ada felt proud and excited. She loved reading stories. Now she could help the fairy tale friends go home.

illustration.description: Ada beams with pride while Ms. Chen and Mr. Rodriguez look at her admiringly in the warm, magical library. Books with glowing pages float gently around them, and golden sparkles fill the air. The mood is celebratory and hopeful.
illustration.alt: Ada feeling proud with the supportive adults in the magical library

pages.text: Soon, all the fairy tale characters were in the library. The Three Bears sat together. Goldilocks and Jack stood near the art books. Cinderella held her glass slipper tightly. Ada opened the first book. "Once upon a time," she began, "there were three bears who lived in the forest..." As she read, the bears began to glow softly.

illustration.description: Ada stands confidently reading from a large, glowing storybook while all the fairy tale characters sit in a circle in the magical library. The Three Bears glow with warm golden light as they listen. The scene is enchanting with floating books and sparkles everywhere.
illustration.alt: Ada reading to all the fairy tale characters in the library

pages.text: "We remember now!" said Papa Bear happily. The three bears walked toward their open book and stepped inside! They waved goodbye from the pages. Next, Ada read about Goldilocks. "Once upon a time, a curious girl found a house in the forest..." Goldilocks glowed and followed the bears into her book. "Thank you, Ada!" she called.

illustration.description: The Three Bears wave happily from inside the pages of their open storybook, which glows with warm light. Goldilocks walks toward her own glowing book while Ada continues reading. The library is filled with magical golden sparkles and wonder.

pages.text: Ada read Jack's story next. He climbed down the beanstalk, glowing brightly, and jumped into his book. Finally, she read Cinderella's tale. "Once upon a time, a kind girl went to a royal ball..." Cinderella put on her glass slipper and curtsied to Ada. "You're a true friend," she said, then stepped into her book. The library was quiet again. All the books were back on their shelves. "You did it, Ada!" said Carlos and Maya together. Ada smiled. It had been the best school day ever!

illustration.description: Cinderella in her beautiful blue ballgown curtsies gracefully to Ada while glowing with magical light, then steps into her open storybook. The library looks peaceful again with all books properly shelved. Ada, Carlos, and Maya celebrate together with big smiles.
illustration.alt: Cinderella thanking Ada before returning to her book

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What did Ada find on the school steps?
questions.explanation: Ada found Cinderella's glass slipper on the front steps of the school.

questions.question: Who did Carlos see in the cafeteria?
questions.explanation: Carlos saw the three bears eating porridge in the school cafeteria.

questions.question: Where was the beanstalk growing?
questions.explanation: Maya saw the giant beanstalk growing through the art room window.

questions.question: How did Ada help the characters get home?
questions.explanation: Ada read each character's story out loud, which helped them remember where they belonged and return to their books.

questions.question: What happened to the characters when Ada read their stories?
questions.explanation: When Ada read their stories, the fairy tale characters began to glow softly and remembered their homes.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.instruction: Fill in the missing words from Ada's adventure!
pages.text: Ada found a glass _____ on the school steps. Her friend Carlos saw three _____ in the cafeteria. Maya discovered a giant _____ growing through the art room window. The fairy tale characters were lost and needed to find their way back to their _____.

pages.title: Ada's Solution
pages.instruction: Help Ada remember how she solved the problem!
pages.text: Ada had a brilliant _____! She decided to _____ the fairy tale stories out loud. When the characters heard their own stories, they began to _____ softly. One by one, they stepped back into their _____ and returned home safely.
